methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Cellares is a Series C biotech company founded by Fabian Gerlinghaus and Omar Kurdi, having raised $350M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, including Series C comparables Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), Colossal Biosciences ($1.5B), and New Limit ($810M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. The Stage-Progression method, using total raised as a proxy base ($12M) and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $20M. The Peer-Median method across the four same-stage, same-sector peers produces $1.9B, with a range spanning $810M to $4.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, though the sector median P/S multiple is 6.1x across three public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $17M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $985M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the Stage-Progression method relies on an indirect proxy for base value, while the Peer-Median approach is more grounded but sensitive to peer selection and stage comparability. The discrepancy between methods suggests the need for additional revenue or unit economics data to narrow the range and clarify where within the peer distribution Cellares most appropriately sits.
